title: ShellCommandHook implementation
purpose: This is the bridge between config-driven hooks and the trait-based dispatch system. ShellCommandHook is a single struct that implements all 11 hook traits, spawning an external process for each hook invocation. It handles the stdin/stdout JSON protocol, exit code interpretation, timeout enforcement, matcher filtering, and the fire-and-forget semantics for session event shell hooks. After this brief, config entries can be translated into registered hooks.
task: Create crates/norn/src/integration/hooks/shell.rs with ShellCommandHook struct. Implement all 11 hook traits (5 existing + 6 new). Each trait method: check matcher, build HookInput, spawn sh -c, pipe stdin, enforce timeout, interpret exit code, parse HookOutput, return HookOutcome. Add fire-and-forget dispatch for session event shell hooks via tokio::spawn. Add non-reentrant guard on HookRegistry session event dispatch. Update hooks/mod.rs.

requirements.title: ShellCommandHook struct
requirements.spec: ShellCommandHook SHALL hold a command string, a compiled HookMatcher, a timeout Duration, the HookEventType it targets, and a set of common context fields (session_id, cwd, agent_id, profile_name) for HookInput construction.
acceptance: ShellCommandHook struct with fields: command (String), matcher (HookMatcher), timeout (Duration), event_type (HookEventType), context (HookContext or equivalent holding session_id, cwd, agent_id, profile_name)
Constructor: new(command, matcher, timeout, event_type, context) -> Self
No Default implementation (all fields are meaningful)

requirements.title: Process spawn and stdin pipe
requirements.spec: WHEN a ShellCommandHook fires, it SHALL spawn sh -c <command> via tokio::process::Command, pipe HookInput JSON to the child's stdin, and set the 5 NORN_* environment variables on the child process.
acceptance: Child process spawned via Command::new("sh").arg("-c").arg(&self.command)
HookInput serialized to JSON string via serde_json::to_string
JSON written to child stdin before stdin is dropped
NORN_PROJECT_DIR, NORN_SESSION_ID, NORN_AGENT_ID, NORN_PROFILE, NORN_HOOK_EVENT set via .env()
Test: shell hook receives JSON on stdin (e.g. command = 'cat > /tmp/test.json', verify file contents)

requirements.title: Timeout enforcement
requirements.spec: WHEN the child process does not complete within the configured timeout, the ShellCommandHook SHALL kill the child process and return HookOutcome::Proceed with a warning logged.
acceptance: tokio::time::timeout wraps wait_with_output()
On timeout: child.kill() called, warning logged with command and timeout duration
HookOutcome::Proceed returned on timeout (non-blocking failure)
Test: command = 'sleep 60' with 1s timeout, verify Proceed returned within ~1s

requirements.title: Exit code protocol
requirements.spec: WHEN exit code is 0, stdout SHALL be parsed as HookOutput JSON and mapped to HookOutcome. WHEN exit code is 2, HookOutcome::Block SHALL be returned with stderr as reason. WHEN exit code is any other value, HookOutcome::Proceed SHALL be returned with stderr logged at warn level.
acceptance: Exit 0: parse stdout as HookOutput, call to_hook_outcome(self.event_type)
Exit 0 with empty stdout: HookOutcome::Proceed (no JSON to parse)
Exit 0 with invalid JSON stdout: HookOutcome::Proceed, warning logged
Exit 2: HookOutcome::Block { reason: stderr_string }
Exit 2 with empty stderr: Block with empty reason string
Other exit codes: HookOutcome::Proceed, stderr logged at warn
Test: script exits 0 with '{"decision":"block","reason":"test"}' → Block
Test: script exits 2 with 'denied' on stderr → Block { reason: 'denied' }
Test: script exits 1 → Proceed with warning
Test: script exits 0 with empty stdout → Proceed

requirements.title: Matcher check before spawn
requirements.spec: WHEN a ShellCommandHook fires for an event type that supports matchers, it SHALL check the matcher against the event's matcher input BEFORE spawning the child process. IF the matcher does not match, the hook SHALL return HookOutcome::Proceed immediately without spawning.
acceptance: Pre-tool hook checks matcher against envelope.tool_name
Pre-LLM hook checks matcher against request.model
Session event hook checks matcher against event variant name
No-matcher events (user_prompt, stop, etc.) skip the matcher check
Non-matching events return Proceed without spawning a process
Test: ShellCommandHook with matcher 'Write' does not fire for tool name 'Read'

requirements.title: Implement all 11 hook traits
requirements.spec: ShellCommandHook SHALL implement all 11 hook traits: PreToolHook, PostToolHook, PreLlmHook, PostLlmHook, SessionEventHook, UserPromptHook, StopHook, SubagentHook, SessionLifecycleHook, CompactionHook, PostToolFailureHook. Each implementation SHALL follow the same pattern: check matcher, build HookInput, execute, interpret result.
acceptance: impl PreToolHook for ShellCommandHook — builds tool-event HookInput, returns Proceed/Block/Modify
impl PostToolHook for ShellCommandHook — builds post-tool HookInput, ignores output (observational)
impl PreLlmHook for ShellCommandHook — builds LLM HookInput, returns Proceed/Block
impl PostLlmHook for ShellCommandHook — builds LLM HookInput, ignores output
impl SessionEventHook for ShellCommandHook — fire-and-forget (see R7)
impl UserPromptHook for ShellCommandHook — builds prompt HookInput, returns Proceed/Block
impl StopHook for ShellCommandHook — builds stop HookInput, returns Proceed/Block
impl SubagentHook for ShellCommandHook — builds subagent HookInput, start is observational, stop returns Proceed/Block
impl SessionLifecycleHook for ShellCommandHook — observational
impl CompactionHook for ShellCommandHook — returns Proceed/Block
impl PostToolFailureHook for ShellCommandHook — builds failure HookInput, observational

requirements.title: Fire-and-forget session event shell hooks
requirements.spec: WHEN a ShellCommandHook implements SessionEventHook, the on_event method SHALL spawn the shell command via tokio::spawn with timeout rather than awaiting it inline. Trait-based SessionEventHook implementations (non-shell) SHALL continue to dispatch synchronously.
acceptance: ShellCommandHook's SessionEventHook::on_event uses tokio::spawn internally
The spawned task has its own timeout enforcement
on_event returns immediately without waiting for the child process
Existing trait-based SessionEventHook dispatch in HookRegistry remains synchronous (awaited in order)
Test: slow shell session event hook does not block the calling async context

requirements.title: Non-reentrant session event dispatch guard
requirements.spec: HookRegistry SHALL have a non-reentrant guard on run_on_event that prevents recursive dispatch. IF run_on_event is called while already inside a run_on_event dispatch, the inner call SHALL skip hook dispatch silently.
acceptance: HookRegistry gains a dispatching_session_events: AtomicBool field (or equivalent)
run_on_event checks and sets the flag before dispatching, clears after
Nested calls while flag is set skip dispatch and return immediately
Flag is always cleared even if a hook panics (guard pattern)
Test: SessionEventHook that calls append_and_notify (triggering run_on_event) does not recurse

boundaries: SHALL NOT load settings files — that is NH-003 (loader) and NH-006 (CLI wiring)
SHALL NOT wire dispatch points for new traits into the agent loop — that is NH-007
SHALL NOT modify existing hook dispatch methods in tool_dispatch.rs or runner.rs beyond what NH-001 already changed
SHALL NOT add todo!() or unimplemented!() macros anywhere
verification: ShellCommandHook implements all 11 hook traits — verified by cargo check (trait bounds)
Exit code 0/2/other all produce correct HookOutcome — verified by test
Matcher filtering prevents unnecessary process spawns — verified by test
Non-reentrant guard prevents recursive session event dispatch — verified by test
No file exceeds 500 lines of code (excluding tests, comments, whitespace)
